questions.question: 1. Oil samples are soluble in which solvent-

explanations.a: Incorrect. Oils are non-polar and insoluble in water (polar).
explanations.b: Correct. Hexane is a non-polar solvent and oils are soluble in it.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Methanol is polar and oils are not soluble in it.
explanations.d: Incorrect. While benzene is non-polar, hexane is the more commonly used solvent for oil extraction.

explanations.a: Incorrect. Monoesters have one fatty acid attached to glycerol.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Diesters have two fatty acids attached to glycerol.
explanations.c: Correct. Fats and oils are triglycerides (triesters of glycerol with three fatty acid molecules).
explanations.d: Incorrect. Glycol esters are not fats or oils.

questions.question: 3. Liquid oils can be converted to solid fats by-

explanations.a: Correct. Hydrogenation adds hydrogen to unsaturated fatty acids, converting liquid oils to solid fats (e.g., vanaspati ghee).
explanations.b: Incorrect. Saponification is hydrolysis of oils with alkali to form soap.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Hydrolysis breaks down oils into fatty acids and glycerol.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Oxidation of double bonds causes rancidity, not solidification.

explanations.a: Correct. Waxes are nonpolar solids composed of long-chain fatty acid esters with long-chain alcohols.
explanations.b: Incorrect. A wax is an ester of a long-chain alcohol and a fatty acid, not just an alcohol.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Triacylglycerols are fats and oils, not waxes.

questions.question: 5. Alkaline hydrolysis of oils (or fats) is called-

explanations.a: Correct. Saponification is the alkaline hydrolysis of fats and oils to produce soap and glycerol.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Fermentation is microbial breakdown of carbohydrates.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Diazotisation is a reaction involving diazonium salts.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Rancidification is oxidative degradation of fats.
